---
title: "Market Gap Analysis: Urban Mobility for Aging Populations"
source: explore-domains
filed: 2026-03-15
section: problem-definition
---

## The Untapped Market

Urban transportation systems worldwide are designed for able-bodied commuters aged 18-55. The 65+ demographic -- projected to reach 1.6 billion globally by 2050 -- faces systematic exclusion from modern mobility solutions. Ride-sharing apps assume smartphone proficiency, micro-mobility requires physical agility, and public transit accessibility remains inconsistent.

## Evidence of the Gap

Research across 12 major cities reveals that seniors make 40% fewer discretionary trips than younger adults, not due to reduced desire for mobility but due to infrastructure barriers. Healthcare appointment attendance drops 23% among seniors without reliable door-to-door transportation. Social isolation costs healthcare systems an estimated $6.7 billion annually in the United States alone.

## Opportunity Frame

The intersection of autonomous vehicle technology, voice-first interfaces, and predictive scheduling creates a window for purpose-built senior mobility platforms. Unlike general-purpose ride-sharing adapted for accessibility, a ground-up design can optimize for comfort, reliability, and trust -- the three factors seniors rank above speed and cost in transportation surveys.
